summary: Tested catalog expansion from 1 to 4 products (card-1..card-4) and the generic data-driven EnvelopePreview refactor. Backend: updated test_printalaram_api.py for 4-product catalog, 2 categories, layout config assertions, and customization/order creation for all new productIds - all 26 tests pass. Frontend: homepage grid shows all 4 cards with correct names/prices/ratings; card-1 regression confirmed (no photo upload, text well positioned); card-2/3/4 customize pages show photo upload UI and name/family text positions correctly with no overlap of baked-in static text (e.g. card-3's 'With Warm Regards' header) and good contrast. Product detail slide toggle works for card-3. Full order flow (customize->checkout->COD->home redirect->WhatsApp toast) verified working for card-4. Mobile (390px) grid and customize page render without overflow. FOUND A VISUAL DESIGN ISSUE: for all 3 new products (card-2, card-3, card-4), the configured 'photo' rectangle in layout does not fully cover the baked-in illustrated sample-couple/person graphic already present in the artwork - uploading a real photo results in the uploaded image only partially covering the illustration, leaving parts of the illustrated person(s) visible around/behind the uploaded photo box (see screenshots). This is a visual overlap issue on the photo area specifically (text positioning itself is fine).

ui_bugs.issue: layout.photo top/left/width/height does not align with/fully cover the baked-in illustrated sample-couple graphic in wedding-green-1.jpg, wedding-brown-1.jpg, wedding-orange-1.jpg artwork. After uploading a real photo, parts of the illustrated person(s) remain visible outside the uploaded photo box, causing a visual clash (illustration + real photo both partially visible).

issues: Photo rectangle size/position should be widened/adjusted (or the baked illustration should be removed from source art) to fully cover the illustrated couple so uploaded photos don't visually clash with leftover illustration edges

action_items: Adjust layout.photo rect (top/left/width/height) for card-2, card-3, card-4 in catalog.py so it fully covers the illustrated sample-couple/person graphic baked into wedding-green-1.jpg / wedding-brown-1.jpg / wedding-orange-1.jpg - currently the photo box only partially overlaps, leaving illustration edges visible around uploaded photos (verified via screenshots at /app/.screenshots/customize_card2.png, customize_card3.png, customize_card4.png)
critical_code_review_comments: EnvelopePreview.js refactor to be fully data-driven via layout prop is clean, well-scoped, and file sizes are within limits.
catalog.py layout config is readable; each product's photo rect just needs re-measurement against the actual artwork's illustrated-couple bounding box.

Name/family text positioning is correctly tuned for all 4 products - no overlap with static baked text (verified card-3's 'With Warm Regards' vs name/family, and card-1 regression) and text contrast/readability is good on all backgrounds (text-shadow used consistently).
No console errors observed during testing besides benign Next.js dev warnings.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (photo-rect visual overlap issue found; all other flows/pages pass)

test_credentials: None required - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_-prefixed customizations and one COD order (card-4, TEST_Vikram Patel/TEST_Patel) via UI flow during testing - not cleaned up (isolated dev-DB test data).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Confirmed: (1) GET /api/products returns exactly 4 products with correct photoUploadEnabled flags and layout configs (backend 100% pass), (2) homepage grid shows all 4 cards correctly, (3) card-1 regression intact - no photo UI, text positioned well, (4) card-2/3/4 customize pages show photo upload + correctly-positioned name/family text with good contrast and no text-overlap issues, (5) full COD order flow verified for card-4 end-to-end including WhatsApp redirect toast, (6) mobile 390px layout has no overflow. ONE VISUAL ISSUE found and not fixed (needs main agent's design judgment): the photo-rectangle placement for card-2/3/4 doesn't fully mask the illustrated sample-couple graphic baked into the source artwork images, so uploaded photos appear to partially overlap/clash with leftover illustration. Screenshots saved under /app/.screenshots/ (customize_card2.png, customize_card3.png, customize_card4.png) for reference.
rca_of_the_issue: The layout.photo dict values (top/left/width/height) for card-2/3/4 in catalog.py were likely eyeballed against the overall card design but not precisely measured against the exact pixel bounding box of the illustrated sample-couple/person graphic already printed in the artwork JPGs. Fix requires either (a) re-measuring/adjusting the photo rect to fully cover the illustration bounding box, or (b) requesting/using cleaner source artwork without a baked-in illustrated person that needs full coverage.
